label_user_symptom: customers experienced issues with network connectivity, manifesting as long network latency and/or timeouts when attempting to connect to resources hosted in Azure regions, as well as other Microsoft services including Microsoft 365 and Power Platform.
label_root_cause: This unqualified change led to a chain of events which culminated in the widespread impact of this incident. In this instance, even though the engineer only had access to a single router, it was still connected to the rest of the Microsoft WAN via the IGP protocol. Therefore, the change resulted in two cascading events. First, routers within the Microsoft global network started recomputing IP connectivity throughout the entire internal network. Second, because of the first event, BGP routers started to readvertise and validate prefixes that we receive from the Internet.
pred_user_symptom: customers experienced issues with network connectivity, manifesting as long network latency and/or timeouts when attempting to connect to resources hosted in Azure regions, as well as other Microsoft services including Microsoft 365 and Power Platform.
pred_root_cause: a network engineer was performing an operational task to add network capacity to the global Wide Area Network (WAN) in Madrid. The task included steps to modify the IP address for each new router, and integration into the IGP and BGP routing domains. The SOP was changed prior to the scheduled event, and the change was not re-tested and did not include proper post-checks, leading to widespread impact.
